title: Tempting Earthy Vietnamese Peaches Banh Cuon
description: A Whole30-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring peaches and a mother sauce base, served in a donabe vessel with a cheese-pull effect, providing an energizing and earthy flavor experience.
instructions: Prepare the donabe vessel by heating it over medium heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour and coconut milk to make the banh cuon batter.
Add sliced peaches to the batter and mix well.
Pour the batter into the donabe vessel and cook for 2-3 minutes or until the bottom is lightly browned.
Add grilled pork, mushrooms, and mother sauce on one half of the banh cuon.
Fold the other half of the banh cuon over the filling to create a half-moon shape.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the other side is lightly browned.
Brush the banh cuon with sesame oil and season with salt and pepper.
Garnish with chopped scallions and serve immediately.
